entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: A cereal crop [Pennisetum glaucum (L.) R. Br.] grown in arid and semi-arid regions of Africa and Asia

entities.name: AFRICA
entities.description: Continent where pearl millet is commonly grown

entities.name: ASIA
entities.description: Continent where pearl millet is commonly grown

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: Country where pearl millet is cultivated on approximately 7.5 million hectares

entities.name: SUB-SAHELIAN AFRICA
entities.description: Region where pearl millet serves as a staple food

entities.name: WEST AFRICA
entities.description: Region where high yielding hybrids and varieties of pearl millet have been evolved

entities.name: ARID AND SEMI-ARID REGIONS
entities.description: Environmental conditions where pearl millet is commonly grown

entities.name: POOR INFERTILE SOILS
entities.description: Soil conditions in which pearl millet can be cultivated

entities.name: DROUGHT PRONE ENVIRONMENTS
entities.description: Environments where pearl millet can survive while other cereal crops cannot

entities.name: PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The measure of grain production, often expressed as yield per unit area

entities.name: GRAIN PRODUCTION
entities.description: The total amount of grain produced, noted as 9.7 Mt in India

entities.name: YIELD PER UNIT AREA
entities.description: The metric used to measure productivity in plant breeding programs

entities.name: PLANT BREEDING PROGRAMS
entities.description: Programs aimed at improving the productivity of grains

entities.name: GENETIC IMPROVEMENT
entities.description: The process of evolving high yielding hybrids and varieties to increase productivity

entities.name: LIVESTOCK
entities.description: Animals for which pearl millet is an important source of fodder or feed

entities.name: POULTRY
entities.description: Birds for which pearl millet is an important source of fodder or feed

entities.name: FODDER
entities.description: The use of pearl millet as feed for livestock and poultry
